model_label: Cursor agent visible runtime; exact provider model not exposed

artifact.true_job: A Chinese Gaokao-style essay for the real 2026 National I prompt, showing how the meaning of one word changed during youth growth and why that change has personal and era significance.

run_history.original_note: The earlier five-round run was recorded honestly: strict-grading and student-expression reached PASS 120, while argument returned REVISE 112.
run_history.rerun_note: A targeted revision fixed the argument bridge and repetitive conclusion issues, then fresh final reviewer instances all returned PASS 120.

facets.name: strict-grading
facets.mission: Protect Gaokao prompt fit, constraints, word choice, growth imprint, complete essay form, and no obvious template writing.
facets.distinctness_rationale: The essay can be logically coherent and student-like while still failing the exact Gaokao prompt or constraints.

facets.name: argument
facets.mission: Protect reasoning support, paragraph progression, transitions, era connection, and non-repetitive non-slogan conclusion.
facets.distinctness_rationale: The essay can fit the prompt and sound natural while still relying on weak logic, a hard-pasted era paragraph, or repetitive conclusion.

facets.name: student-expression
facets.mission: Protect credible high-school student voice, concrete experience, natural tone, restrained emotion, and non-template expression.
facets.distinctness_rationale: The essay can be correct and well-argued while still sounding too adult, promotional, or formulaic for a student exam essay.

evidence_items.description: Real 2026 National I Gaokao essay prompt source is recorded and cross-checked.
Original failed argument review retained as historical evidence, not used for the new full_pass claim.
Fresh final reviewers all include Verdict PASS, Score 120, no must-fix issues, evidence checked, and 120-level approval statements.

metrics.token_estimate_note: Estimated from visible text only; not billing token usage.
